name: Timberborn
description: Humans are long gone. In a world struck by droughts and toxic waste, will your lumberpunk beavers do any better? A sandbox city-building game featuring ingenious animals, vertical architecture, water physics, and terraforming. Contains high amounts of wood.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: Windows 10 or newer
  • Processor: 4-core 1.7 GHz or better
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: GeForce GTX 950, Radeon R9 380 or similar
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Storage: 3 GB available space
comment1:
So this game's city planning and water mechanics are awesome, but the game pretty quickly falls into tedium. Once you can divert a bad tide, you've basically won, even on harder difficulties (just a matter of scale at that point).

I would love if there was a longer term challenge, or a different event, or literally anything challenging in the late game that would make it a more dynamic/interesting to play between playthroughs.
comment2: This is objectively one of the best sim city type games out there. My only issue is the wonky in-game tutorial which is really just a bunch of popups that help get you started and they don't guide you all the way.
comment3: Very fun game. It is easy to start with and figure out how to progress. The whole premise of the game is just the cherry on the top as well. A lot of easter eggs and concept. Developers release content at a very healthy pace too. They never fail to deliver too! Highly recommend
